market_summary: S&P 500 closed at 7,041.28 on April 17 — first-ever close above 7,000 (+3.29% for the week April 11-17). A further ~1.3% surge estimated on April 18 on Iran Strait of Hormuz reopening news (~7,133 estimated close, ~+4.63% weekly total). Nasdaq +5.2% for the week, 12th consecutive positive session — longest win streak since 2009. Iran declared Strait of Hormuz completely open on April 17; oil fell ~11%. US naval blockade technically remains in force; ceasefire violated by both sides; no formal peace deal yet. AVGO surged +28% in April 2026 on new AI partnership contracts with Google, Meta, and Anthropic including a 3.5GW compute infrastructure deal.
macro_themes: Fed held at 3.50-3.75%; April 28-29 FOMC widely expected to hold (JPMorgan: 'an easy call to stay on hold'). PCE inflation forecast revised UP to 2.7% for 2026. March CPI +3.3% YoY (highest since April 2024) driven by Iran war energy costs; gasoline +18.9% YoY. Expected to peak ~3.6-4% headline in April-May then decline toward 3% by year-end as Hormuz reopening reverses energy prices — sets up H2 2026 rate cut window. Core CPI well-contained at +2.6% YoY. YTD 2026 sector rotation: Energy +21%, Materials +17%, Staples +15%, Industrials +12% outperforming; this specific week saw Nasdaq lead +5.2% on geopolitical risk resolution. Powell succession uncertainty (term expires May 2026) remains a live policy risk. AVGO +28% in April on AI custom silicon demand surge.
portfolio_health: Grade: C+ (75/100). NAV $4,315.88, +2.13% week vs S&P 500 +3.29% = underperformance of 1.16pp entirely attributable to 11.6% idle cash drag. The CSPX $250 ADD has now been recommended for EIGHT consecutive reports without execution — this remains the portfolio's singular critical operational failure. All 7 positions are in the green. ASML improved to +4.49% unrealized (up from +0.98% last week at $1,410 vs $1,459.80 now). AMZN at +17.13% is the strongest single-stock position. WHT structure is clean: only $3.33 withheld YTD. YTD since inception (April 4 seed $4,191): +2.98%. Key upcoming catalysts: MA earnings April 23, FOMC April 28-29, AMZN earnings April 29.

positions.rationale: 26.2% of total NAV vs 36% structural target — 9.8pp underweight. S&P 500 at all-time highs (7,041.28 on April 17). ADD $250 at Monday April 20 open. EIGHTH consecutive report flagging this as CRITICAL. Post-trade weight rises to ~31.8% of NAV; cash falls from 11.6% to ~5.8%.

Q1 earnings April 29 with 72 analysts at Strong Buy, $291 avg target (+16% upside from $250.56). Do not add pre-earnings at +17% unrealized gain. Post-earnings beat is the sizing review trigger.

Central bank purchases: 755 tonnes forecast for 2026. 4-week sustained risk-on invalidation clock NOT started — ceasefire fragile, US blockade technically in force, no formal peace deal signed.

ADD $100 if cross-border volume >14% AND VAS growth >20% AND guidance maintained. HOLD if in-line. Evaluate partial exit if miss or guidance cut. Analyst consensus: 35/38 Buy, $655 avg target (+25% upside).

Blackwell sold out through mid-2026 (supply-constrained). $1T orders confirmed as actual purchase orders at GTC 2026. Data center revenue +75% YoY, 88% of business. Do not add at elevated levels; reassess on pullback to $180-185. Next catalyst: Q1 FY2027 earnings ~May 20, 2026.
